events.text: Kenneth Allen, 57-year-old White male, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the Base of tongue. Clinical staging: T2N2aM0, AJCC Stage III. ICD-10: C10.9. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 nephrotoxicity. Aggressive IV hydration. Creatinine monitored. Dose adjustment per CrCl.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
MRI neck with contrast performed for restaging after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Clinical visit for results review after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Initiated first-line for recurrent/metastatic: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Cisplatin + 5-FU. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 3 mucositis. Magic mouthwash (lidocaine/diphenhydramine/Maalox) QID. PEG tube placed for nutritional support.
Initiated first-line for recurrent/metastatic: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Cisplatin + 5-FU. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 3 mucositis. Magic mouthwash (lidocaine/diphenhydramine/Maalox) QID. PEG tube placed for nutritional support.
Initiated first-line for recurrent/metastatic: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Cisplatin + 5-FU. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 3 mucositis. Magic mouthwash (lidocaine/diphenhydramine/Maalox) QID. PEG tube placed for nutritional support.
Initiated first-line for recurrent/metastatic: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Cisplatin + 5-FU. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
CT neck with contrast performed for restaging after first-line for recurrent/metastatic.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line for recurrent/metastatic.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT neck with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT neck with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Allen
MRN: patient_c8b7663f7c4e
Date of Study: 2023-05-02

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Allen
MRN: patient_c8b7663f7c4e
Specimen ID: S2023-17539
Date of Procedure: 2023-05-05
Date of Report: 2023-05-09

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.0 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Kenneth Allen - Base of tongue" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 4.0 x 2.8 x 2.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Radiation Oncology

Patient: Kenneth Allen
MRN: patient_c8b7663f7c4e
Date: 2023-05-08
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Allen is a 57-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue, III. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. Pathology confirmed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated). Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports abdominal discomfort and early satiety.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- COPD

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Appendectomy (age 25)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Retired teacher
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: unintentional weight loss, intermittent nausea, mild peripheral edema. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 143/65, HR 82, RR 15, Temp 98.6F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 99.4 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.3, Hgb 10.2, Plt 165, Cr 1.17

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
57-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. initial oncology consultation.

1. Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue, Stage III
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend definitive concurrent chemoradiation
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Radiation Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Allen
MRN: patient_c8b7663f7c4e
Date of Report: 2023-05-16
Specimen: Base of tongue (Resection)
Diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated)
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.7 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  HPV HPV-16 positive (p16 IHC positive) - Viral [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  NOTCH1 p.P2164fs - Frameshift (Exon 34)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CASP8 p.R127* - VUS
  FAT1 p.G4551S - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - HPV-positive disease: Generally favorable prognosis. De-escalation trials may be appropriate.
  - PIK3CA mutation: Alpelisib under investigation in HNSCC.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 472x coverage.
Tumor content: 22%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

MRI NECK WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Kenneth Allen
MRN: patient_c8b7663f7c4e
Date of Study: 2023-08-09

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. restaging after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
OROPHARYNX: Enhancing mass centered in the base of tongue measuring 3.6 x 2.5 cm with extension to the soft palate.
NECK: Level III lymphadenopathy on the right, largest node 3.0 cm with necrotic center.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.
